column1: Ex3A_reviewingSurveyResults.txt.
1. What conclusion can you reach about the responses to this question?
* I concluded making the answer choices specifically or adding categories for not making confusion.
2. Is the default summary or visualization helpful? How could it be improved?
* It is helpful for just overview, but it could be improved by adding categories more clearly and helpful.
3. For questions with an “Other” option, where can you find the actual text of those
responses? Do you notice anything that might be tricky about analyzing these?
* It is found in responses view. Yes, it is tricky analyzing written responses is difficult through differences in words.
4. For questions calling for a written response, what challenges do you notice about
finding trends or patterns in the answers provided?
* It’s kind of challenging because it requires reading each individual to understand trends.
5. Now that you can see the responses, how might you recommend rephrasing this or
question or the response options in a future survey?
* It could be improved with clear comparisons
Next respond to the below questions:
6. Is there anything that surprised you about the survey responses?
* Some of the answers were similar.
7. Looking at the responses to questions 1 and 5, are there any ideas you might try to
include in your morning routine, or any questions that you are also curious about?
* Some new ideas and time management
Year Up United Data Analyst Training Academy Week 1 Lab Workbook
Page 14 of 55
8. Pretend you are the instructor. Is there anything you can learn from this survey to help
in how you approach teaching this class?
* I could learn students may have challenges too and which could help guide when I teach in class.
9. Again, pretend you are the instructor. Do you think it was helpful or not helpful to
make this survey anonymous? Why or why not? (If you can see both pros and cons,
you may include both.)
On the Responses Overview page for the form, under “Insights and actions”, click on the
button to open the survey responses in Excel. Take a moment to review the layout of the
data. Then at the top click on File > Create a Copy > Download a Copy to download the
responses to your Data Analytics/week-01 folder as Exercise 1.A Getting Set Up.xlsx
When you are finished
* I think its helpful because people are more honest when they don’t have to put their name.
